<objective>
Integrate markdown export UI throughout the app with single-note export, bulk export with folder structure, and task export capabilities.

Purpose: Enable users to easily export their notes and tasks to markdown files through intuitive UI buttons and menu actions.
Output: Export buttons in Notes page, Tasks page, and Settings with download functionality and user feedback.
</objective>

<tasks>

<task type="auto">
  <name>Task 1: Add export UI to Notes page</name>
  <files>src/pages/Notes.tsx, src/components/ExportNotesModal.tsx</files>
  <action>
    Integrate markdown export into Notes page:

    1. **Add Export button to Notes page header:**
       - Place next to existing "New Note" and "New Note from Template" buttons
       - Icon: Download or FileDown (lucide-react)
       - Label: "Export"
       - Opens ExportNotesModal on click

    2. **Create src/components/ExportNotesModal.tsx:**

       **Export options:**
       - **Single note:** Export currently selected note (if activeNoteId exists)
       - **Selected notes:** Export all notes in current selection (if multi-select enabled)
       - **Current folder:** Export all notes in current folder with folder structure
       - **All notes:** Export all notes (including all folders, full hierarchy)

       **UI structure:**
       - Radio buttons for export scope (single/selected/folder/all)
       - Checkbox: "Include folder structure" (disabled for single note)
       - Checkbox: "Export as ZIP" (enabled for multi-note exports, required for folder structure)
       - Preview: "X notes will be exported" (dynamic count based on selection)
       - Format dropdown: "Markdown (.md)" (only option for now, future: PDF, HTML)
       - Export button: "Export Notes"
       - Cancel button

       **Export flow:**
       - Single note: Convert to markdown → download as "note-title.md"
       - Multiple notes (no folder structure): Convert all → download as "notes-export-DATE.zip"
       - Multiple notes (with folder structure): Convert all → preserve hierarchy → download as "neuman-brain-notes-DATE.zip"
       - Show toast notification: "X notes exported successfully" or "Export failed: [error]"

       **Progress indicator:**
       - For large exports (>50 notes), show progress bar: "Exporting... 25/100 notes"
       - Use setTimeout batching to avoid blocking UI (process 10 notes at a time)

    3. **Add context menu export:**
       - Right-click on note in list → "Export to Markdown" option
       - Exports that specific note immediately (no modal)
       - Toast: "Note exported: note-title.md"

    4. **Keyboard shortcut:**
       - Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+E to open ExportNotesModal
       - Document in ShortcutsWidget

    5. **Pattern to follow:** src/components/TemplateLibrary.tsx (similar modal pattern)

    6. **Avoid:**
       - Blocking UI during large exports (use batching)
       - Downloading without user confirmation (always show preview count)
       - Exporting archived notes by default (add "Include archived" checkbox)

    7. **Why:** Users need granular control over what they export - sometimes single note, sometimes entire workspace. Folder structure preservation is critical for organization.

    Research: https://www.nngroup.com/articles/progress-indicators/ (progress feedback UX)
  </action>
  <verify>
    - Export button visible in Notes page header
    - ExportNotesModal opens on click
    - Single note export downloads .md file correctly
    - Bulk export creates ZIP with all notes
    - Folder structure preserved in ZIP when checkbox enabled
    - Right-click context menu shows "Export to Markdown"
    - Cmd/Ctrl+Shift+E opens modal
    - Toast notifications appear on success/failure
    - Progress bar shows for exports >50 notes
  </verify>
  <done>Export UI in Notes page functional, ExportNotesModal working, folder structure preserved, context menu integrated, keyboard shortcut documented</done>
</task>

<task type="auto">
  <name>Task 2: Add export UI to Tasks page</name>
  <files>src/pages/Tasks.tsx, src/components/ExportTasksModal.tsx</files>
  <action>
    Integrate markdown export into Tasks/Kanban page:

    1. **Add Export button to Tasks page:**
       - Place in toolbar (near existing bulk actions)
       - Icon: Download or FileDown
       - Label: "Export"
       - Opens ExportTasksModal

    2. **Create src/components/ExportTasksModal.tsx:**

       **Export options:**
       - **Single task:** Export currently selected task (if card detail panel open)
       - **Column:** Export all tasks in current column
       - **Board:** Export all tasks across all columns
       - **Status group:** Export all tasks with specific status (backlog/todo/inprogress/done)

       **UI structure:**
       - Radio buttons for export scope
       - Format options:
         - "Separate files by status" (one .md file per status column)
         - "Single file (all tasks)" (one .md file with all tasks grouped by status)
       - Checkbox: "Include archived tasks" (default: false)
       - Checkbox: "Export as ZIP" (enabled for multi-file exports)
       - Preview: "X tasks will be exported across Y files"
       - Export button: "Export Tasks"
       - Cancel button

       **Export flow:**
       - Single task: Convert to markdown → download as "task-title.md"
       - By status: Create separate files (backlog.md, todo.md, inprogress.md, review.md, done.md)
       - All tasks: Single file "all-tasks.md" with table of contents
       - Show toast: "X tasks exported successfully"

       **Task markdown format:**
       - Use exportTaskToMarkdown() from markdownExport.ts
       - Include frontmatter with metadata
       - Preserve checklist items, comments, dependencies

    3. **Add context menu export:**
       - Right-click on task card → "Export to Markdown"
       - Exports that specific task immediately
       - Toast: "Task exported: task-title.md"

    4. **Add bulk export action:**
       - When tasks selected (multi-select mode), show "Export selected" in toolbar
       - Exports only selected tasks → ZIP if >1 task

    5. **Pattern to follow:** ExportNotesModal.tsx (consistent UX)

    6. **Avoid:**
       - Exporting sensitive task data without user awareness (comments, assignees should be opt-in checkbox)
       - Losing task metadata during export (preserve all fields in frontmatter)
       - Overwhelming users with too many options (keep export flow simple)

    7. **Why:** Tasks have different organizational structure than notes (columns/status vs folders). Need specialized export options matching task workflow.

    Research: https://github.com/obsidianmd/obsidian-releases/blob/master/plugin-guidelines.md (markdown export patterns)
  </action>
  <verify>
    - Export button visible in Tasks page toolbar
    - ExportTasksModal opens on click
    - Single task export downloads .md file with frontmatter
    - Separate files by status creates backlog.md, todo.md, etc.
    - All tasks export creates single file with table of contents
    - Right-click context menu shows "Export to Markdown"
    - Bulk export works for selected tasks
    - Toast notifications appear on success/failure
    - Archived tasks excluded by default (unless checkbox enabled)
  </verify>
  <done>Export UI in Tasks page functional, ExportTasksModal working, status-based export options available, bulk export integrated</done>
</task>

<task type="auto">
  <name>Task 3: Add global export to Settings page</name>
  <files>src/pages/Settings.tsx, src/widgets/Settings/ExportSettings.tsx</files>
  <action>
    Add comprehensive export settings to Settings page:

    1. **Create src/widgets/Settings/ExportSettings.tsx:**

       **Section title:** "Data Export"

       **Description:** "Export your notes, tasks, and data to portable markdown format for backup, sharing, or migration."

       **Export options:**
       - **Export All Notes:**
         - Button: "Export All Notes to Markdown"
         - Downloads ZIP with full folder hierarchy
         - Count: "X notes across Y folders"
       - **Export All Tasks:**
         - Button: "Export All Tasks to Markdown"
         - Downloads ZIP with tasks grouped by status
         - Count: "X tasks (Y archived)"
       - **Export Everything:**
         - Button: "Export Complete Workspace"
         - Downloads ZIP with notes + tasks + metadata.json
         - Structure:
           ```
           neuman-brain-export-DATE.zip/
             ├── notes/ (full hierarchy)
             ├── tasks/
             │   ├── by-status/
             │   └── all-tasks.md
             └── metadata.json
           ```
         - Count: "X notes, Y tasks, Z folders"

       **Export format info:**
       - Collapsible "Export Format Details" section
       - Explains frontmatter YAML structure
       - Shows example note markdown
       - Shows example task markdown
       - Links to documentation (if exists)

       **Export history (future enhancement placeholder):**
       - List of recent exports (timestamp, file count, download link)
       - Stored in localStorage: last 5 exports
       - Clear history button

    2. **Integrate into Settings page:**
       - Add ExportSettings widget to Data section
       - Place after "Backup & Restore" settings (if exists) or at bottom of Data section

    3. **Progress feedback:**
       - For large exports, show progress toast: "Exporting... X/Y items processed"
       - Disable export buttons during export (prevent double-click)
       - Re-enable after completion or error

    4. **Error handling:**
       - If export fails: Show error toast with reason ("Not enough memory", "Browser blocked download")
       - Offer "Retry" button in toast
       - Log error to console for debugging

    5. **Pattern to follow:** src/widgets/Settings/DailyNotesSettings.tsx (similar settings widget)

    6. **Avoid:**
       - Exporting without confirmation for large datasets (show count first)

# Phase 1: Foundation Summary

**JWT auth with refresh rotation using jose library, Prisma User model, and protected API middleware**

## Accomplishments
- User model with email/password auth
- Login/logout endpoints with httpOnly JWT cookies
- Protected route middleware checking token validity

### Auto-fixed Issues

**1. [Rule 2 - Missing Critical] Added password hashing with bcrypt**
- **Found during:** Task 2 (Login endpoint implementation)

**2. [Rule 3 - Blocking] Installed missing jose dependency**

- **Issue:** jose package not in package.json, import failing
- **Fix:** Ran `npm install jose`
- **Files modified:** package.json, package-lock.json
- **Verification:** Import succeeds, build passes
- **Commit:** def456g

### Deferred Enhancements

Logged to .planning/ISSUES.md for future consideration:
- ISS-001: Add rate limiting to login endpoint (discovered in Task 2)
- ISS-002: Improve token refresh UX with auto-retry on 401 (discovered in Task 5)

---

**Total deviations:** 2 auto-fixed (1 missing critical, 1 blocking), 2 deferred
**Impact on plan:** Both auto-fixes essential for security and functionality. No scope creep.

## Issues Encountered
- jsonwebtoken CommonJS import failed in Edge runtime - switched to jose (planned library change, worked as expected)

## Next Phase Readiness
- Auth foundation complete, ready for feature development
- User registration endpoint needed before public launch

---
*Phase: 01-foundation*
*Completed: 2025-01-15*

1→# Workflow: Execute Phase
     2→
     3→<purpose>
     4→Execute a phase prompt (PLAN.md) and create the outcome summary (SUMMARY.md).
     5→</purpose>
     6→
     7→<process>
     8→
     9→<step name="identify_plan">
    10→Find the next plan to execute:
    11→- Check ROADMAP.md for "In progress" phase
    12→- Find plans in that phase directory
    13→- Identify first plan without corresponding SUMMARY
    14→
    15→```bash
    16→cat .planning/ROADMAP.md
    17→# Look for phase with "In progress" status
    18→# Then find plans in that phase
    19→ls .planning/phases/XX-name/*-PLAN.md 2>/dev/null | sort
    20→ls .planning/phases/XX-name/*-SUMMARY.md 2>/dev/null | sort
    21→```
    22→
    23→**Logic:**
    24→- If `01-01-PLAN.md` exists but `01-01-SUMMARY.md` doesn't → execute 01-01
    25→- If `01-01-SUMMARY.md` exists but `01-02-SUMMARY.md` doesn't → execute 01-02
    26→- Pattern: Find first PLAN file without matching SUMMARY file
    27→
    28→Confirm with user if ambiguous.
    29→
    30→Present:
    31→```
    32→Found plan to execute: {phase}-{plan}-PLAN.md
    33→[Plan X of Y for Phase Z]
    34→
    35→Proceed with execution?
    36→```
    37→</step>
    38→
    39→<step name="parse_segments">
    40→**Intelligent segmentation: Parse plan into execution segments.**
    41→
    42→Plans are divided into segments by checkpoints. Each segment is routed to optimal execution context (subagent or main).
    43→
    44→**1. Check for checkpoints:**
    45→```bash
    46→# Find all checkpoints and their types
    47→grep -n "type=\"checkpoint" .planning/phases/XX-name/{phase}-{plan}-PLAN.md
    48→```
    49→
    50→**2. Analyze execution strategy:**
    51→
    52→**If NO checkpoints found:**
    53→- **Fully autonomous plan** - spawn single subagent for entire plan
    54→- Subagent gets fresh 200k context, executes all tasks, creates SUMMARY, commits
    55→- Main context: Just orchestration (~5% usage)
    56→
    57→**If checkpoints found, parse into segments:**
    58→
    59→Segment = tasks between checkpoints (or start→first checkpoint, or last checkpoint→end)
    60→
    61→**For each segment, determine routing:**
    62→
    63→```
    64→Segment routing rules:
    65→
    66→IF segment has no prior checkpoint:
    67→  → SUBAGENT (first segment, nothing to depend on)
    68→
    69→IF segment follows checkpoint:human-verify:
    70→  → SUBAGENT (verification is just confirmation, doesn't affect next work)
    71→
    72→IF segment follows checkpoint:decision OR checkpoint:human-action:
    73→  → MAIN CONTEXT (next tasks need the decision/result)
    74→```
    75→
    76→**3. Execution pattern:**
    77→
    78→**Pattern A: Fully autonomous (no checkpoints)**
    79→```
    80→Spawn subagent → execute all tasks → SUMMARY → commit → report back
    81→```
    82→
    83→**Pattern B: Segmented with verify-only checkpoints**
    84→```
    85→Segment 1 (tasks 1-3): Spawn subagent → execute → report back
    86→Checkpoint 4 (human-verify): Main context → you verify → continue
    87→Segment 2 (tasks 5-6): Spawn NEW subagent → execute → report back
    88→Checkpoint 7 (human-verify): Main context → you verify → continue
    89→Aggregate results → SUMMARY → commit
    90→```
    91→
    92→**Pattern C: Decision-dependent (must stay in main)**
    93→```
    94→Checkpoint 1 (decision): Main context → you decide → continue in main
    95→Tasks 2-5: Main context (need decision from checkpoint 1)
    96→No segmentation benefit - execute entirely in main
    97→```
    98→
    99→**4. Why this works:**
   100→
   101→**Segmentation benefits:**
   102→- Fresh context for each autonomous segment (0% start every time)
   103→- Main context only for checkpoints (~10-20% total)
   104→- Can handle 10+ task plans if properly segmented
   105→- Quality impossible to degrade in autonomous segments
   106→
   107→**When segmentation provides no benefit:**
   108→- Checkpoint is decision/human-action and following tasks depend on outcome
   109→- Better to execute sequentially in main than break flow
   110→
   111→**5. Implementation:**
   112→
   113→**For fully autonomous plans:**
   114→```
   115→Use Task tool with subagent_type="general-purpose":
   116→
   117→Prompt: "Execute plan at .planning/phases/{phase}-{plan}-PLAN.md
   118→
   119→This is an autonomous plan (no checkpoints). Execute all tasks, create SUMMARY.md in phase directory, commit with message following plan's commit guidance.
   120→
   121→Follow all deviation rules and authentication gate protocols from the plan.
   122→
   123→When complete, report: plan name, tasks completed, SUMMARY path, commit hash."
   124→```
   125→
   126→**For segmented plans (has verify-only checkpoints):**
   127→```
   128→Execute segment-by-segment:
   129→
   130→For each autonomous segment:
   131→  Spawn subagent with prompt: "Execute tasks [X-Y] from plan at .planning/phases/{phase}-{plan}-PLAN.md. Read the plan for full context and deviation rules. Do NOT create SUMMARY or commit - just execute these tasks and report results."
   132→
   133→  Wait for subagent completion
   134→
   135→For each checkpoint:
   136→  Execute in main context
   137→  Wait for user interaction
   138→  Continue to next segment
   139→
   140→After all segments complete:
   141→  Aggregate all results
   142→  Create SUMMARY.md
   143→  Commit with all changes
   144→```
   145→
   146→**For decision-dependent plans:**
   147→```
   148→Execute in main context (standard flow below)
   149→No subagent routing
   150→Quality maintained through small scope (2-3 tasks per plan)
   151→```
   152→
   153→See step name="segment_execution" for detailed segment execution loop.
   154→</step>
   155→
   156→<step name="segment_execution">
   157→**Detailed segment execution loop for segmented plans.**
   158→
   159→**This step applies ONLY to segmented plans (Pattern B: has checkpoints, but they're verify-only).**
   160→
   161→For Pattern A (fully autonomous) and Pattern C (decision-dependent), skip this step.
   162→
   163→**Execution flow:**
   164→
   165→```
   166→1. Parse plan to identify segments:
   167→   - Read plan file
   168→   - Find checkpoint locations: grep -n "type=\"checkpoint" PLAN.md
   169→   - Identify checkpoint types: grep "type=\"checkpoint" PLAN.md | grep -o 'checkpoint:[^"]*'
   170→   - Build segment map:
   171→     * Segment 1: Start → first checkpoint (tasks 1-X)
   172→     * Checkpoint 1: Type and location
   173→     * Segment 2: After checkpoint 1 → next checkpoint (tasks X+1 to Y)
   174→     * Checkpoint 2: Type and location
   175→     * ... continue for all segments
   176→
   177→2. For each segment in order:
   178→
   179→   A. Determine routing (apply rules from parse_segments):
   180→      - No prior checkpoint? → Subagent
   181→      - Prior checkpoint was human-verify? → Subagent
   182→      - Prior checkpoint was decision/human-action? → Main context
   183→
   184→   B. If routing = Subagent:
   185→      ```
   186→      Spawn Task tool with subagent_type="general-purpose":
   187→
   188→      Prompt: "Execute tasks [task numbers/names] from plan at [plan path].
   189→
   190→      **Context:**
   191→      - Read the full plan for objective, context files, and deviation rules
   192→      - You are executing a SEGMENT of this plan (not the full plan)
   193→      - Other segments will be executed separately
   194→
   195→      **Your responsibilities:**
   196→      - Execute only the tasks assigned to you
   197→      - Follow all deviation rules and authentication gate protocols
   198→      - Track deviations for later Summary
   199→      - DO NOT create SUMMARY.md (will be created after all segments complete)
   200→      - DO NOT commit (will be done after all segments complete)
   201→
   202→      **Report back:**
   203→      - Tasks completed
   204→      - Files created/modified
   205→      - Deviations encountered
   206→      - Any issues or blockers"
   207→
   208→      Wait for subagent to complete
   209→      Capture results (files changed, deviations, etc.)
   210→      ```
   211→
   212→   C. If routing = Main context:
   213→      Execute tasks in main using standard execution flow (step name="execute")
   214→      Track results locally
   215→
   216→   D. After segment completes (whether subagent or main):
   217→      Continue to next checkpoint/segment
   218→
   219→3. After ALL segments complete:
   220→
   221→   A. Aggregate results from all segments:
   222→      - Collect files created/modified from all segments
   223→      - Collect deviations from all segments
   224→      - Collect decisions from all checkpoints
   225→      - Merge into complete picture
   226→
   227→   B. Create SUMMARY.md:
   228→      - Use aggregated results
   229→      - Document all work from all segments
   230→      - Include deviations from all segments
   231→      - Note which segments were subagented
   232→
   233→   C. Commit:
   234→      - Stage all files from all segments
   235→      - Stage SUMMARY.md
   236→      - Commit with message following plan guidance
   237→      - Include note about segmented execution if relevant
   238→
   239→   D. Report completion
   240→
   241→**Example execution trace:**
   242→
   243→```
   244→Plan: 01-02-PLAN.md (8 tasks, 2 verify checkpoints)
   245→
   246→Parsing segments...
   247→- Segment 1: Tasks 1-3 (autonomous)
   248→- Checkpoint 4: human-verify
   249→- Segment 2: Tasks 5-6 (autonomous)
   250→- Checkpoint 7: human-verify
   251→- Segment 3: Task 8 (autonomous)
   252→
   253→Routing analysis:
   254→- Segment 1: No prior checkpoint → SUBAGENT ✓
   255→- Checkpoint 4: Verify only → MAIN (required)
   256→- Segment 2: After verify → SUBAGENT ✓
   257→- Checkpoint 7: Verify only → MAIN (required)
   258→- Segment 3: After verify → SUBAGENT ✓
   259→
   260→Execution:
   261→[1] Spawning subagent for tasks 1-3...
   262→    → Subagent completes: 3 files modified, 0 deviations
   263→[2] Executing checkpoint 4 (human-verify)...
   264→    ════════════════════════════════════════
   265→    CHECKPOINT: Verification Required
   266→    Task 4 of 8: Verify database schema
   267→    I built: User and Session tables with relations
   268→    How to verify: Check src/db/schema.ts for correct types
   269→    ════════════════════════════════════════
   270→    User: "approved"
   271→[3] Spawning subagent for tasks 5-6...
   272→    → Subagent completes: 2 files modified, 1 deviation (added error handling)
   273→[4] Executing checkpoint 7 (human-verify)...
   274→    User: "approved"
   275→[5] Spawning subagent for task 8...
   276→    → Subagent completes: 1 file modified, 0 deviations
   277→
   278→Aggregating results...
   279→- Total files: 6 modified
   280→- Total deviations: 1
   281→- Segmented execution: 3 subagents, 2 checkpoints
   282→
   283→Creating SUMMARY.md...
   284→Committing...
   285→✓ Complete
   286→```
   287→
   288→**Benefits of this pattern:**
   289→- Main context usage: ~20% (just orchestration + checkpoints)
   290→- Subagent 1: Fresh 0-30% (tasks 1-3)
   291→- Subagent 2: Fresh 0-30% (tasks 5-6)
   292→- Subagent 3: Fresh 0-20% (task 8)
   293→- All autonomous work: Peak quality
   294→- Can handle large plans with many tasks if properly segmented
   295→
   296→**When NOT to use segmentation:**

   359→<authentication_gates>
   360→## Handling Authentication Errors During Execution
   361→
   362→**When you encounter authentication errors during `type="auto"` task execution:**
   363→
   364→This is NOT a failure. Authentication gates are expected and normal. Handle them dynamically:
   365→
   366→**Authentication error indicators:**
   367→- CLI returns: "Error: Not authenticated", "Not logged in", "Unauthorized", "401", "403"
   368→- API returns: "Authentication required", "Invalid API key", "Missing credentials"
   369→- Command fails with: "Please run {tool} login" or "Set {ENV_VAR} environment variable"
   370→
   371→**Authentication gate protocol:**
   372→
   373→1. **Recognize it's an auth gate** - Not a bug, just needs credentials
   374→2. **STOP current task execution** - Don't retry repeatedly
   375→3. **Create dynamic checkpoint:human-action** - Present it to user immediately
   376→4. **Provide exact authentication steps** - CLI commands, where to get keys
   377→5. **Wait for user to authenticate** - Let them complete auth flow
   378→6. **Verify authentication works** - Test that credentials are valid
   379→7. **Retry the original task** - Resume automation where you left off
   380→8. **Continue normally** - Don't treat this as an error in Summary
   381→
   382→**Example: Vercel deployment hits auth error**
   383→
   384→```
   385→Task 3: Deploy to Vercel
   386→Running: vercel --yes
   387→
   388→Error: Not authenticated. Please run 'vercel login'
   389→
   390→[Create checkpoint dynamically]
   391→
   392→════════════════════════════════════════
   393→CHECKPOINT: Authentication Required
   394→════════════════════════════════════════
   395→
   396→Task 3 of 8: Authenticate Vercel CLI
   397→
   398→I tried to deploy but got authentication error.
   399→
   400→What you need to do:
   401→Run: vercel login
   402→
   403→This will open your browser - complete the authentication flow.
   404→
   405→I'll verify after: vercel whoami returns your account
   406→
   407→Type "done" when authenticated
   408→════════════════════════════════════════
   409→
   410→[Wait for user response]
   411→
   412→[User types "done"]
   413→
   414→Verifying authentication...
   415→Running: vercel whoami
   416→✓ Authenticated as: user@example.com
   417→
   418→Retrying deployment...
   419→Running: vercel --yes
   420→✓ Deployed to: https://myapp-abc123.vercel.app
   421→
   422→Task 3 complete. Continuing to task 4...
   423→```
   424→
   425→**Example: Stripe API needs key**
   426→
   427→```
   428→Task 5: Create Stripe webhook endpoint
   429→Using Stripe API...
   430→
   431→Error: 401 Unauthorized - No API key provided
   432→
   433→[Create checkpoint dynamically]
   434→
   435→════════════════════════════════════════
   436→CHECKPOINT: Credentials Required
   437→════════════════════════════════════════
   438→
   439→Task 5 of 8: Provide Stripe API key
   440→
   441→I tried to create webhook but need your Stripe API key.
   442→
   443→What you need to do:
   444→1. Visit dashboard.stripe.com/apikeys
   445→2. Copy your "Secret key" (starts with sk_test_ or sk_live_)
   446→3. Paste it here, or run: export STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=sk_...
   447→
   448→I'll verify after: Stripe API call succeeds
   449→
   450→Type "done" when ready, or paste the key
   451→════════════════════════════════════════
   452→
   453→[Wait for user response]
   454→
   455→[User pastes key or exports env var]
   456→
   457→Saving key to .env...
   458→Verifying Stripe API access...
   459→✓ Stripe API authenticated
   460→
   461→Retrying webhook creation...
   462→✓ Webhook endpoint created: whsec_abc123
   463→
   464→Task 5 complete. Continuing to task 6...
   465→```
   466→
   467→**In Summary documentation:**
   468→
   469→Document authentication gates as normal flow, not deviations:
   470→
   471→```markdown
   472→## Authentication Gates
   473→
   474→During execution, I encountered authentication requirements:
   475→
   476→1. Task 3: Vercel CLI required authentication
   477→   - Paused for `vercel login`
   478→   - Resumed after authentication
   479→   - Deployed successfully
   480→
   481→2. Task 5: Stripe API required API key
   482→   - Paused for API key input
   483→   - Saved to .env
   484→   - Resumed webhook creation
   485→
   486→These are normal gates, not errors.
   487→```
   488→
   489→**Key principles:**
   490→- Authentication gates are NOT failures or bugs
   491→- They're expected interaction points during first-time setup
   492→- Handle them gracefully and continue automation after unblocked
   493→- Don't mark tasks as "failed" or "incomplete" due to auth gates
   494→- Document them as normal flow, separate from deviations
   495→
   496→See references/cli-automation.md "Authentication Gates" section for complete examples.
   497→</authentication_gates>

   501→<deviation_rules>
   502→## Automatic Deviation Handling
   503→
   504→**While executing tasks, you WILL discover work not in the plan.** This is normal.
   505→
   506→Apply these rules automatically. Track all deviations for Summary documentation.
   507→
   508→---
   509→
   510→**RULE 1: Auto-fix bugs**
   511→
   512→**Trigger:** Code doesn't work as intended (broken behavior, incorrect output, errors)
   513→
   514→**Action:** Fix immediately, track for Summary
   515→
   516→**Examples:**
   517→- Wrong SQL query returning incorrect data
   518→- Logic errors (inverted condition, off-by-one, infinite loop)
   519→- Type errors, null pointer exceptions, undefined references
   520→- Broken validation (accepts invalid input, rejects valid input)
   521→- Security vulnerabilities (SQL injection, XSS, CSRF, insecure auth)
   522→- Race conditions, deadlocks
   523→- Memory leaks, resource leaks
   524→
   525→**Process:**
   526→1. Fix the bug inline
   527→2. Add/update tests to prevent regression
   528→3. Verify fix works
   529→4. Continue task
   530→5. Track in deviations list: `[Rule 1 - Bug] [description]`
   531→
   532→**No user permission needed.** Bugs must be fixed for correct operation.
   533→
   534→---
   535→
   536→**RULE 2: Auto-add missing critical functionality**
   537→
   538→**Trigger:** Code is missing essential features for correctness, security, or basic operation
   539→
   540→**Action:** Add immediately, track for Summary
   541→
   542→**Examples:**
   543→- Missing error handling (no try/catch, unhandled promise rejections)
   544→- No input validation (accepts malicious data, type coercion issues)
   545→- Missing null/undefined checks (crashes on edge cases)
   546→- No authentication on protected routes
   547→- Missing authorization checks (users can access others' data)
   548→- No CSRF protection, missing CORS configuration
   549→- No rate limiting on public APIs
   550→- Missing required database indexes (causes timeouts)
   551→- No logging for errors (can't debug production)
   552→
   553→**Process:**
   554→1. Add the missing functionality inline
   555→2. Add tests for the new functionality
   556→3. Verify it works
   557→4. Continue task
   558→5. Track in deviations list: `[Rule 2 - Missing Critical] [description]`
   559→
   560→**Critical = required for correct/secure/performant operation**
   561→**No user permission needed.** These are not "features" - they're requirements for basic correctness.
   562→
   563→---
   564→
   565→**RULE 3: Auto-fix blocking issues**
   566→
   567→**Trigger:** Something prevents you from completing current task
   568→
   569→**Action:** Fix immediately to unblock, track for Summary
   570→
   571→**Examples:**
   572→- Missing dependency (package not installed, import fails)
   573→- Wrong types blocking compilation
   574→- Broken import paths (file moved, wrong relative path)
   575→- Missing environment variable (app won't start)
   576→- Database connection config error
   577→- Build configuration error (webpack, tsconfig, etc.)
   578→- Missing file referenced in code
   579→- Circular dependency blocking module resolution
   580→
   581→**Process:**
   582→1. Fix the blocking issue
   583→2. Verify task can now proceed
   584→3. Continue task
   585→4. Track in deviations list: `[Rule 3 - Blocking] [description]`
   586→
   587→**No user permission needed.** Can't complete task without fixing blocker.
   588→
   589→---
   590→
   591→**RULE 4: Ask about architectural changes**
   592→
   593→**Trigger:** Fix/addition requires significant structural modification
   594→
   595→**Action:** STOP, present to user, wait for decision
   596→
   597→**Examples:**
   598→- Adding new database table (not just column)
   599→- Major schema changes (changing primary key, splitting tables)
   600→- Introducing new service layer or architectural pattern
   601→- Switching libraries/frameworks (React → Vue, REST → GraphQL)
   602→- Changing authentication approach (sessions → JWT)
   603→- Adding new infrastructure (message queue, cache layer, CDN)
   604→- Changing API contracts (breaking changes to endpoints)
   605→- Adding new deployment environment
   606→
   607→**Process:**
   608→1. STOP current task
   609→2. Present clearly:
   610→```
   611→⚠️ Architectural Decision Needed
   612→
   613→Current task: [task name]
   614→Discovery: [what you found that prompted this]
   615→Proposed change: [architectural modification]
   616→Why needed: [rationale]
   617→Impact: [what this affects - APIs, deployment, dependencies, etc.]
   618→Alternatives: [other approaches, or "none apparent"]
   619→
   620→Proceed with proposed change? (yes / different approach / defer)
   621→```
   622→3. WAIT for user response
   623→4. If approved: implement, track as `[Rule 4 - Architectural] [description]`
   624→5. If different approach: discuss and implement
   625→6. If deferred: log to ISSUES.md, continue without change
   626→
   627→**User decision required.** These changes affect system design.
   628→
   629→---
   630→
   631→**RULE 5: Log non-critical enhancements**
   632→
   633→**Trigger:** Improvement that would enhance code but isn't essential now
   634→
   635→**Action:** Add to .planning/ISSUES.md automatically, continue task
   636→
   637→**Examples:**
   638→- Performance optimization (works correctly, just slower than ideal)
   639→- Code refactoring (works, but could be cleaner/DRY-er)
   640→- Better naming (works, but variables could be clearer)
   641→- Organizational improvements (works, but file structure could be better)
   642→- Nice-to-have UX improvements (works, but could be smoother)
   643→- Additional test coverage beyond basics (basics exist, could be more thorough)
   644→- Documentation improvements (code works, docs could be better)
   645→- Accessibility enhancements beyond minimum
   646→
   647→**Process:**
   648→1. Create .planning/ISSUES.md if doesn't exist (use template)
   649→2. Add entry with ISS-XXX number (auto-increment)
   650→3. Brief notification: `📋 Logged enhancement: [brief] (ISS-XXX)`
   651→4. Continue task without implementing
   652→
   653→**Template for ISSUES.md:**
   654→```markdown
   655→# Project Issues Log
   656→
   657→Enhancements discovered during execution. Not critical - address in future phases.
   658→
   659→## Open Enhancements
   660→
   661→### ISS-001: [Brief description]
   662→- **Discovered:** Phase [X] Plan [Y] Task [Z] (YYYY-MM-DD)
   663→- **Type:** [Performance / Refactoring / UX / Testing / Documentation / Accessibility]
   664→- **Description:** [What could be improved and why it would help]
   665→- **Impact:** Low (works correctly, this would enhance)
   666→- **Effort:** [Quick / Medium / Substantial]
   667→- **Suggested phase:** [Phase number or "Future"]
   668→
   669→## Closed Enhancements
   670→
   671→[Moved here when addressed]
   672→```
   673→
   674→**No user permission needed.** Logging for future consideration.
   675→
   676→---
   677→
   678→**RULE PRIORITY (when multiple could apply):**
   679→
   680→1. **If Rule 4 applies** → STOP and ask (architectural decision)
   681→2. **If Rules 1-3 apply** → Fix automatically, track for Summary
   682→3. **If Rule 5 applies** → Log to ISSUES.md, continue
   683→4. **If genuinely unsure which rule** → Apply Rule 4 (ask user)
   684→
   685→**Edge case guidance:**
   686→- "This validation is missing" → Rule 2 (critical for security)
   687→- "This validation could be better" → Rule 5 (enhancement)
   688→- "This crashes on null" → Rule 1 (bug)
   689→- "This could be faster" → Rule 5 (enhancement) UNLESS actually timing out → Rule 2 (critical)
   690→- "Need to add table" → Rule 4 (architectural)
   691→- "Need to add column" → Rule 1 or 2 (depends: fixing bug or adding critical field)
   692→
   693→**When in doubt:** Ask yourself "Does this affect correctness, security, or ability to complete task?"
   694→- YES → Rules 1-3 (fix automatically)
   695→- NO → Rule 5 (log it)
   696→- MAYBE → Rule 4 (ask user)
   697→
   698→</deviation_rules>

# Workflow: Execute Phase

<purpose>
Execute a phase prompt (PLAN.md) and create the outcome summary (SUMMARY.md).
</purpose>

<process>

<step name="identify_plan">
Find the next plan to execute:
- Check ROADMAP.md for "In progress" phase
- Find plans in that phase directory
- Identify first plan without corresponding SUMMARY

```bash
cat .planning/ROADMAP.md
# Look for phase with "In progress" status
# Then find plans in that phase
ls .planning/phases/XX-name/*-PLAN.md 2>/dev/null | sort
ls .planning/phases/XX-name/*-SUMMARY.md 2>/dev/null | sort
```

**Logic:**
- If `01-01-PLAN.md` exists but `01-01-SUMMARY.md` doesn't → execute 01-01
- If `01-01-SUMMARY.md` exists but `01-02-SUMMARY.md` doesn't → execute 01-02
- Pattern: Find first PLAN file without matching SUMMARY file

Confirm with user if ambiguous.

Present:
```
Found plan to execute: {phase}-{plan}-PLAN.md
[Plan X of Y for Phase Z]

Proceed with execution?
```
</step>

<step name="parse_segments">
**Intelligent segmentation: Parse plan into execution segments.**

Plans are divided into segments by checkpoints. Each segment is routed to optimal execution context (subagent or main).

**1. Check for checkpoints:**
```bash
# Find all checkpoints and their types
grep -n "type=\"checkpoint" .planning/phases/XX-name/{phase}-{plan}-PLAN.md
```

**2. Analyze execution strategy:**

**If NO checkpoints found:**
- **Fully autonomous plan** - spawn single subagent for entire plan
- Subagent gets fresh 200k context, executes all tasks, creates SUMMARY, commits
- Main context: Just orchestration (~5% usage)

**If checkpoints found, parse into segments:**

Segment = tasks between checkpoints (or start→first checkpoint, or last checkpoint→end)

**For each segment, determine routing:**

```
Segment routing rules:

IF segment has no prior checkpoint:
  → SUBAGENT (first segment, nothing to depend on)

IF segment follows checkpoint:human-verify:
  → SUBAGENT (verification is just confirmation, doesn't affect next work)

IF segment follows checkpoint:decision OR checkpoint:human-action:
  → MAIN CONTEXT (next tasks need the decision/result)
```

**3. Execution pattern:**

**Pattern A: Fully autonomous (no checkpoints)**
```
Spawn subagent → execute all tasks → SUMMARY → commit → report back
```

**Pattern B: Segmented with verify-only checkpoints**
```
Segment 1 (tasks 1-3): Spawn subagent → execute → report back
Checkpoint 4 (human-verify): Main context → you verify → continue
Segment 2 (tasks 5-6): Spawn NEW subagent → execute → report back
Checkpoint 7 (human-verify): Main context → you verify → continue
Aggregate results → SUMMARY → commit
```

**Pattern C: Decision-dependent (must stay in main)**
```
Checkpoint 1 (decision): Main context → you decide → continue in main
Tasks 2-5: Main context (need decision from checkpoint 1)
No segmentation benefit - execute entirely in main
```

**4. Why this works:**

**Segmentation benefits:**
- Fresh context for each autonomous segment (0% start every time)
- Main context only for checkpoints (~10-20% total)
- Can handle 10+ task plans if properly segmented
- Quality impossible to degrade in autonomous segments

**When segmentation provides no benefit:**
- Checkpoint is decision/human-action and following tasks depend on outcome
- Better to execute sequentially in main than break flow

**5. Implementation:**

**For fully autonomous plans:**
```
Use Task tool with subagent_type="general-purpose":

Prompt: "Execute plan at .planning/phases/{phase}-{plan}-PLAN.md

This is an autonomous plan (no checkpoints). Execute all tasks, create SUMMARY.md in phase directory, commit with message following plan's commit guidance.

Follow all deviation rules and authentication gate protocols from the plan.

When complete, report: plan name, tasks completed, SUMMARY path, commit hash."
```

**For segmented plans (has verify-only checkpoints):**
```
Execute segment-by-segment:

For each autonomous segment:
  Spawn subagent with prompt: "Execute tasks [X-Y] from plan at .planning/phases/{phase}-{plan}-PLAN.md. Read the plan for full context and deviation rules. Do NOT create SUMMARY or commit - just execute these tasks and report results."

  Wait for subagent completion

For each checkpoint:
  Execute in main context
  Wait for user interaction
  Continue to next segment

After all segments complete:
  Aggregate all results
  Create SUMMARY.md
  Commit with all changes
```

**For decision-dependent plans:**
```
Execute in main context (standard flow below)
No subagent routing
Quality maintained through small scope (2-3 tasks per plan)
```

See step name="segment_execution" for detailed segment execution loop.
</step>

<step name="segment_execution">
**Detailed segment execution loop for segmented plans.**

**This step applies ONLY to segmented plans (Pattern B: has checkpoints, but they're verify-only).**

For Pattern A (fully autonomous) and Pattern C (decision-dependent), skip this step.

**Execution flow:**

```
1. Parse plan to identify segments:
   - Read plan file
   - Find checkpoint locations: grep -n "type=\"checkpoint" PLAN.md
   - Identify checkpoint types: grep "type=\"checkpoint" PLAN.md | grep -o 'checkpoint:[^"]*'
   - Build segment map:
     * Segment 1: Start → first checkpoint (tasks 1-X)
     * Checkpoint 1: Type and location
     * Segment 2: After checkpoint 1 → next checkpoint (tasks X+1 to Y)
     * Checkpoint 2: Type and location
     * ... continue for all segments

2. For each segment in order:

   A. Determine routing (apply rules from parse_segments):
      - No prior checkpoint? → Subagent
      - Prior checkpoint was human-verify? → Subagent
      - Prior checkpoint was decision/human-action? → Main context

   B. If routing = Subagent:
      ```
      Spawn Task tool with subagent_type="general-purpose":

      Prompt: "Execute tasks [task numbers/names] from plan at [plan path].

      **Context:**
      - Read the full plan for objective, context files, and deviation rules
      - You are executing a SEGMENT of this plan (not the full plan)
      - Other segments will be executed separately

      **Your responsibilities:**
      - Execute only the tasks assigned to you
      - Follow all deviation rules and authentication gate protocols
      - Track deviations for later Summary
      - DO NOT create SUMMARY.md (will be created after all segments complete)
      - DO NOT commit (will be done after all segments complete)

      **Report back:**
      - Tasks completed
      - Files created/modified
      - Deviations encountered
      - Any issues or blockers"

      Wait for subagent to complete
      Capture results (files changed, deviations, etc.)
      ```

   C. If routing = Main context:
      Execute tasks in main using standard execution flow (step name="execute")
      Track results locally

   D. After segment completes (whether subagent or main):
      Continue to next checkpoint/segment

3. After ALL segments complete:

   A. Aggregate results from all segments:
      - Collect files created/modified from all segments
      - Collect deviations from all segments
      - Collect decisions from all checkpoints
      - Merge into complete picture

   B. Create SUMMARY.md:
      - Use aggregated results
      - Document all work from all segments
      - Include deviations from all segments
      - Note which segments were subagented

   C. Commit:
      - Stage all files from all segments
      - Stage SUMMARY.md
      - Commit with message following plan guidance
      - Include note about segmented execution if relevant

   D. Report completion

**Example execution trace:**

```
Plan: 01-02-PLAN.md (8 tasks, 2 verify checkpoints)

Parsing segments...
- Segment 1: Tasks 1-3 (autonomous)
- Checkpoint 4: human-verify
- Segment 2: Tasks 5-6 (autonomous)
- Checkpoint 7: human-verify
- Segment 3: Task 8 (autonomous)

Routing analysis:
- Segment 1: No prior checkpoint → SUBAGENT ✓
- Checkpoint 4: Verify only → MAIN (required)
- Segment 2: After verify → SUBAGENT ✓
- Checkpoint 7: Verify only → MAIN (required)
- Segment 3: After verify → SUBAGENT ✓

Execution:
[1] Spawning subagent for tasks 1-3...
    → Subagent completes: 3 files modified, 0 deviations
[2] Executing checkpoint 4 (human-verify)...
    ════════════════════════════════════════
    CHECKPOINT: Verification Required
    Task 4 of 8: Verify database schema
    I built: User and Session tables with relations
    How to verify: Check src/db/schema.ts for correct types
    ════════════════════════════════════════
    User: "approved"
[3] Spawning subagent for tasks 5-6...
    → Subagent completes: 2 files modified, 1 deviation (added error handling)
[4] Executing checkpoint 7 (human-verify)...
    User: "approved"
[5] Spawning subagent for task 8...
    → Subagent completes: 1 file modified, 0 deviations

Aggregating results...
- Total files: 6 modified
- Total deviations: 1
- Segmented execution: 3 subagents, 2 checkpoints

Creating SUMMARY.md...
Committing...
✓ Complete
```

**Benefits of this pattern:**
- Main context usage: ~20% (just orchestration + checkpoints)
- Subagent 1: Fresh 0-30% (tasks 1-3)
- Subagent 2: Fresh 0-30% (tasks 5-6)
- Subagent 3: Fresh 0-20% (task 8)
- All autonomous work: Peak quality
- Can handle large plans with many tasks if properly segmented

**When NOT to use segmentation:**
- Plan has decision/human-action checkpoints that affect following tasks
- Following tasks depend on checkpoint outcome
- Better to execute in main sequentially in those cases
</step>

<step name="load_prompt">
Read the plan prompt:
```bash
cat .planning/phases/XX-name/{phase}-{plan}-PLAN.md
```

This IS the execution instructions. Follow it exactly.
</step>

<step name="previous_phase_check">
Before executing, check if previous phase had issues:

```bash
# Find previous phase summary
ls .planning/phases/*/SUMMARY.md 2>/dev/null | sort -r | head -2 | tail -1
```

If previous phase SUMMARY.md has "Issues Encountered" != "None" or "Next Phase Readiness" mentions blockers:

Use AskUserQuestion:
- header: "Previous Issues"
- question: "Previous phase had unresolved items: [summary]. How to proceed?"
- options:
  - "Proceed anyway" - Issues won't block this phase
  - "Address first" - Let's resolve before continuing
  - "Review previous" - Show me the full summary
</step>

<step name="execute">
Execute each task in the prompt. **Deviations are normal** - handle them automatically using embedded rules below.

1. Read the @context files listed in the prompt

2. For each task:

   **If `type="auto"`:**
   - Work toward task completion
   - **If CLI/API returns authentication error:** Handle as authentication gate (see below)
   - **When you discover additional work not in plan:** Apply deviation rules (see below) automatically
   - Continue implementing, applying rules as needed
   - Run the verification
   - Confirm done criteria met
   - Track any deviations for Summary documentation
   - Continue to next task

   **If `type="checkpoint:*"`:**
   - STOP immediately (do not continue to next task)
   - Execute checkpoint_protocol (see below)
   - Wait for user response
   - Verify if possible (check files, env vars, etc.)
   - Only after user confirmation: continue to next task

3. Run overall verification checks from `<verification>` section
4. Confirm all success criteria from `<success_criteria>` section met
5. Document all deviations in Summary (automatic - see deviation_documentation below)
</step>

<authentication_gates>
## Handling Authentication Errors During Execution

**When you encounter authentication errors during `type="auto"` task execution:**

This is NOT a failure. Authentication gates are expected and normal. Handle them dynamically:

**Authentication error indicators:**
- CLI returns: "Error: Not authenticated", "Not logged in", "Unauthorized", "401", "403"
- API returns: "Authentication required", "Invalid API key", "Missing credentials"
- Command fails with: "Please run {tool} login" or "Set {ENV_VAR} environment variable"

**Authentication gate protocol:**

1. **Recognize it's an auth gate** - Not a bug, just needs credentials
2. **STOP current task execution** - Don't retry repeatedly
3. **Create dynamic checkpoint:human-action** - Present it to user immediately
4. **Provide exact authentication steps** - CLI commands, where to get keys
5. **Wait for user to authenticate** - Let them complete auth flow
6. **Verify authentication works** - Test that credentials are valid
7. **Retry the original task** - Resume automation where you left off
8. **Continue normally** - Don't treat this as an error in Summary

**Example: Vercel deployment hits auth error**

```
Task 3: Deploy to Vercel
Running: vercel --yes

Error: Not authenticated. Please run 'vercel login'

[Create checkpoint dynamically]

════════════════════════════════════════
CHECKPOINT: Authentication Required
════════════════════════════════════════

Task 3 of 8: Authenticate Vercel CLI

I tried to deploy but got authentication error.

What you need to do:
Run: vercel login

This will open your browser - complete the authentication flow.

I'll verify after: vercel whoami returns your account

Type "done" when authenticated
════════════════════════════════════════

[Wait for user response]

[User types "done"]

Verifying authentication...
Running: vercel whoami
✓ Authenticated as: user@example.com

Retrying deployment...
Running: vercel --yes
✓ Deployed to: https://myapp-abc123.vercel.app

Task 3 complete. Continuing to task 4...
```

**Example: Stripe API needs key**

```
Task 5: Create Stripe webhook endpoint
Using Stripe API...

Error: 401 Unauthorized - No API key provided

[Create checkpoint dynamically]

════════════════════════════════════════
CHECKPOINT: Credentials Required
════════════════════════════════════════

Task 5 of 8: Provide Stripe API key

I tried to create webhook but need your Stripe API key.

What you need to do:
1. Visit dashboard.stripe.com/apikeys
2. Copy your "Secret key" (starts with sk_test_ or sk_live_)
3. Paste it here, or run: export STRIPE_SECRET_KEY=sk_...

I'll verify after: Stripe API call succeeds

Type "done" when ready, or paste the key
════════════════════════════════════════

[Wait for user response]

[User pastes key or exports env var]

Saving key to .env...
Verifying Stripe API access...
✓ Stripe API authenticated

Retrying webhook creation...
✓ Webhook endpoint created: whsec_abc123

Task 5 complete. Continuing to task 6...
```

**In Summary documentation:**

Document authentication gates as normal flow, not deviations:

```markdown
## Authentication Gates

During execution, I encountered authentication requirements:

1. Task 3: Vercel CLI required authentication
   - Paused for `vercel login`
   - Resumed after authentication
   - Deployed successfully

2. Task 5: Stripe API required API key
   - Paused for API key input
   - Saved to .env
   - Resumed webhook creation

These are normal gates, not errors.
```

**Key principles:**
- Authentication gates are NOT failures or bugs
- They're expected interaction points during first-time setup
- Handle them gracefully and continue automation after unblocked
- Don't mark tasks as "failed" or "incomplete" due to auth gates
- Document them as normal flow, separate from deviations

See references/cli-automation.md "Authentication Gates" section for complete examples.
</authentication_gates>

<step name="execute">

<deviation_rules>
## Automatic Deviation Handling

**While executing tasks, you WILL discover work not in the plan.** This is normal.

Apply these rules automatically. Track all deviations for Summary documentation.

---

**RULE 1: Auto-fix bugs**

**Trigger:** Code doesn't work as intended (broken behavior, incorrect output, errors)

**Action:** Fix immediately, track for Summary

**Examples:**
- Wrong SQL query returning incorrect data
- Logic errors (inverted condition, off-by-one, infinite loop)
- Type errors, null pointer exceptions, undefined references
- Broken validation (accepts invalid input, rejects valid input)
- Security vulnerabilities (SQL injection, XSS, CSRF, insecure auth)
- Race conditions, deadlocks
- Memory leaks, resource leaks

**Process:**
1. Fix the bug inline
2. Add/update tests to prevent regression
3. Verify fix works
4. Continue task
5. Track in deviations list: `[Rule 1 - Bug] [description]`

**No user permission needed.** Bugs must be fixed for correct operation.

---

**RULE 2: Auto-add missing critical functionality**

**Trigger:** Code is missing essential features for correctness, security, or basic operation

**Action:** Add immediately, track for Summary

**Examples:**
- Missing error handling (no try/catch, unhandled promise rejections)
- No input validation (accepts malicious data, type coercion issues)
- Missing null/undefined checks (crashes on edge cases)
- No authentication on protected routes
- Missing authorization checks (users can access others' data)
- No CSRF protection, missing CORS configuration
- No rate limiting on public APIs
- Missing required database indexes (causes timeouts)
- No logging for errors (can't debug production)

**Process:**
1. Add the missing functionality inline
2. Add tests for the new functionality
3. Verify it works
4. Continue task
5. Track in deviations list: `[Rule 2 - Missing Critical] [description]`

**Critical = required for correct/secure/performant operation**
**No user permission needed.** These are not "features" - they're requirements for basic correctness.

---

**RULE 3: Auto-fix blocking issues**

**Trigger:** Something prevents you from completing current task

**Action:** Fix immediately to unblock, track for Summary

**Examples:**
- Missing dependency (package not installed, import fails)
- Wrong types blocking compilation
- Broken import paths (file moved, wrong relative path)
- Missing environment variable (app won't start)
- Database connection config error
- Build configuration error (webpack, tsconfig, etc.)
- Missing file referenced in code
- Circular dependency blocking module resolution

**Process:**
1. Fix the blocking issue
2. Verify task can now proceed
3. Continue task
4. Track in deviations list: `[Rule 3 - Blocking] [description]`

**No user permission needed.** Can't complete task without fixing blocker.

---

**RULE 4: Ask about architectural changes**

**Trigger:** Fix/addition requires significant structural modification

**Action:** STOP, present to user, wait for decision

**Examples:**
- Adding new database table (not just column)
- Major schema changes (changing primary key, splitting tables)
- Introducing new service layer or architectural pattern
- Switching libraries/frameworks (React → Vue, REST → GraphQL)
- Changing authentication approach (sessions → JWT)
- Adding new infrastructure (message queue, cache layer, CDN)
- Changing API contracts (breaking changes to endpoints)
- Adding new deployment environment

**Process:**
1. STOP current task
2. Present clearly:
```
⚠️ Architectural Decision Needed

Current task: [task name]
Discovery: [what you found that prompted this]
Proposed change: [architectural modification]
Why needed: [rationale]
Impact: [what this affects - APIs, deployment, dependencies, etc.]
Alternatives: [other approaches, or "none apparent"]

Proceed with proposed change? (yes / different approach / defer)
```
3. WAIT for user response
4. If approved: implement, track as `[Rule 4 - Architectural] [description]`
5. If different approach: discuss and implement
6. If deferred: log to ISSUES.md, continue without change

**User decision required.** These changes affect system design.

---

**RULE 5: Log non-critical enhancements**

**Trigger:** Improvement that would enhance code but isn't essential now

**Action:** Add to .planning/ISSUES.md automatically, continue task

**Examples:**
- Performance optimization (works correctly, just slower than ideal)
- Code refactoring (works, but could be cleaner/DRY-er)
- Better naming (works, but variables could be clearer)
- Organizational improvements (works, but file structure could be better)
- Nice-to-have UX improvements (works, but could be smoother)
- Additional test coverage beyond basics (basics exist, could be more thorough)
- Documentation improvements (code works, docs could be better)
- Accessibility enhancements beyond minimum

**Process:**
1. Create .planning/ISSUES.md if doesn't exist (use template)
2. Add entry with ISS-XXX number (auto-increment)
3. Brief notification: `📋 Logged enhancement: [brief] (ISS-XXX)`
4. Continue task without implementing

**Template for ISSUES.md:**
```markdown
# Project Issues Log

Enhancements discovered during execution. Not critical - address in future phases.

## Open Enhancements

### ISS-001: [Brief description]
- **Discovered:** Phase [X] Plan [Y] Task [Z] (YYYY-MM-DD)
- **Type:** [Performance / Refactoring / UX / Testing / Documentation / Accessibility]
- **Description:** [What could be improved and why it would help]
- **Impact:** Low (works correctly, this would enhance)
- **Effort:** [Quick / Medium / Substantial]
- **Suggested phase:** [Phase number or "Future"]

## Closed Enhancements

[Moved here when addressed]
```

**No user permission needed.** Logging for future consideration.

---

**RULE PRIORITY (when multiple could apply):**

1. **If Rule 4 applies** → STOP and ask (architectural decision)
2. **If Rules 1-3 apply** → Fix automatically, track for Summary
3. **If Rule 5 applies** → Log to ISSUES.md, continue
4. **If genuinely unsure which rule** → Apply Rule 4 (ask user)

**Edge case guidance:**
- "This validation is missing" → Rule 2 (critical for security)
- "This validation could be better" → Rule 5 (enhancement)
- "This crashes on null" → Rule 1 (bug)
- "This could be faster" → Rule 5 (enhancement) UNLESS actually timing out → Rule 2 (critical)
- "Need to add table" → Rule 4 (architectural)
- "Need to add column" → Rule 1 or 2 (depends: fixing bug or adding critical field)

**When in doubt:** Ask yourself "Does this affect correctness, security, or ability to complete task?"
- YES → Rules 1-3 (fix automatically)
- NO → Rule 5 (log it)
- MAYBE → Rule 4 (ask user)

</deviation_rules>

<deviation_documentation>
## Documenting Deviations in Summary

After all tasks complete, Summary MUST include deviations section.

**If no deviations:**
```markdown
## Deviations from Plan

None - plan executed exactly as written.
```

**If deviations occurred:**
```markdown
## Deviations from Plan

### Auto-fixed Issues

**1. [Rule 1 - Bug] Fixed case-sensitive email uniqueness constraint**
- **Found during:** Task 4 (Follow/unfollow API implementation)
- **Issue:** User.email unique constraint was case-sensitive - Test@example.com and test@example.com were both allowed, causing duplicate accounts
- **Fix:** Changed to `CREATE UNIQUE INDEX users_email_unique ON users (LOWER(email))`
- **Files modified:** src/models/User.ts, migrations/003_fix_email_unique.sql
- **Verification:** Unique constraint test passes - duplicate emails properly rejected
- **Commit:** abc123f

**2. [Rule 2 - Missing Critical] Added JWT expiry validation to auth middleware**
- **Found during:** Task 3 (Protected route implementation)
- **Issue:** Auth middleware wasn't checking token expiry - expired tokens were being accepted
- **Fix:** Added exp claim validation in middleware, reject with 401 if expired
- **Files modified:** src/middleware/auth.ts, src/middleware/auth.test.ts
- **Verification:** Expired token test passes - properly rejects with 401
- **Commit:** def456g

**3. [Rule 3 - Blocking] Fixed broken import path for UserService**
- **Found during:** Task 5 (Profile endpoint)
- **Issue:** Import path referenced old location (src/services/User.ts) but file was moved to src/services/users/UserService.ts in previous plan
- **Fix:** Updated import path
- **Files modified:** src/api/profile.ts
- **Verification:** Build succeeds, imports resolve
- **Commit:** ghi789h

**4. [Rule 4 - Architectural] Added Redis caching layer (APPROVED BY USER)**
- **Found during:** Task 6 (Feed endpoint)
- **Issue:** Feed queries hitting database on every request, causing 2-3 second response times under load
- **Proposed:** Add Redis cache with 5-minute TTL for feed data
- **User decision:** Approved
- **Fix:** Implemented Redis caching with ioredis client, cache invalidation on new posts
- **Files created:** src/cache/RedisCache.ts, src/cache/CacheKeys.ts, docker-compose.yml (added Redis)
- **Verification:** Feed response time reduced to <200ms, cache hit rate >80% in testing
- **Commit:** jkl012m

### Deferred Enhancements

Logged to .planning/ISSUES.md for future consideration:
- ISS-001: Refactor UserService into smaller modules (discovered in Task 3)
- ISS-002: Add connection pooling for Redis (discovered in Task 6)
- ISS-003: Improve error messages for validation failures (discovered in Task 2)

---

**Total deviations:** 4 auto-fixed (1 bug, 1 missing critical, 1 blocking, 1 architectural with approval), 3 deferred
**Impact on plan:** All auto-fixes necessary for correctness/security/performance. No scope creep.
```

**This provides complete transparency:**
- Every deviation documented
- Why it was needed
- What rule applied
- What was done
- User can see exactly what happened beyond the plan

</deviation_documentation>

<step name="checkpoint_protocol">
When encountering `type="checkpoint:*"`:

**Critical: Claude automates everything with CLI/API before checkpoints.** Checkpoints are for verification and decisions, not manual work.

**Display checkpoint clearly:**
```
════════════════════════════════════════
CHECKPOINT: [Type]
════════════════════════════════════════

Task [X] of [Y]: [Action/What-Built/Decision]

[Display task-specific content based on type]

[Resume signal instruction]
════════════════════════════════════════
```

**For checkpoint:human-verify (90% of checkpoints):**
```
I automated: [what was automated - deployed, built, configured]

How to verify:
1. [Step 1 - exact command/URL]
2. [Step 2 - what to check]
3. [Step 3 - expected behavior]

[Resume signal - e.g., "Type 'approved' or describe issues"]
```

**For checkpoint:decision (9% of checkpoints):**
```
Decision needed: [decision]

Context: [why this matters]

Options:
1. [option-id]: [name]
   Pros: [pros]
   Cons: [cons]

2. [option-id]: [name]
   Pros: [pros]
   Cons: [cons]

[Resume signal - e.g., "Select: option-id"]
```

**For checkpoint:human-action (1% - rare, only for truly unavoidable manual steps):**
```
I automated: [what Claude already did via CLI/API]

Need your help with: [the ONE thing with no CLI/API - email link, 2FA code]

Instructions:
[Single unavoidable step]

I'll verify after: [verification]

# Neuman Brain - Post-P0-P3 Era

## Current State (Updated: 2025-11-30)

**Shipped:** v1.0 Foundation (2025-11-30)
**Status:** Local-first MVP complete, all P0-P3 priorities shipped
**Users:** Internal testing, preparing for beta
**Feedback:** Foundation proving solid - privacy-first architecture working, local-only data integrity maintained
**Codebase:**
- 89,472 lines of TypeScript/React
- React 19 + TypeScript 5.9 + Vite 7
- Zustand state management + IndexedDB persistence
- 128/128 tests passing (79 unit + 49 integration)
- Bundle size: 117KB gzip

**Known Issues:**
- Technical debt: IndexedDB query optimization, full-text search implementation
- UX polish needed across features
- Documentation sparse (user-facing docs minimal)
- Architecture decisions pending (block editor, graph database, local AI, CRDTs)

## v1.1+ Goals

**Vision:** Transform from solid foundation to competitive all-in-one platform while maintaining privacy-first differentiator.

**Motivation:**
- Foundation complete (P0-P3 100%) - ready to build vertically
- Quick wins available (tags, templates, celebrations) - high value, low effort
- Strategic features (collaboration, plugins, mobile) require architecture research
- Quality refinement needed before broad user adoption

**Scope (v1.1-v1.x):**
- Quality & Refinement: E2E tests, technical debt fixes, UX polish, documentation
- Quick Wins: Note tags, templates, task celebrations, natural language dates, markdown export
- P4 Planning: Research real-time collaboration, plugin system, mobile apps, publishing, automation
- Architecture Research: Block editor evaluation, graph database feasibility, local AI models, CRDT library selection

**Success Criteria:**
- [x] All E2E critical flows covered by Playwright tests (completed 2025-11-30 - 98 E2E tests)
- [x] Technical debt addressed (IndexedDB optimization, full-text search, bundle analysis) (completed 2025-11-30)
- [x] User documentation complete (getting started, feature guides, troubleshooting) (completed 2025-12-01 - 7 user guides)
- [ ] Quick wins shipped (5 features: tags, templates, celebrations, NL dates, markdown export)
- [ ] P4 architecture decisions documented with evidence (research findings, POCs, trade-off analysis)
- [ ] Platform ready for beta user onboarding

**Out of Scope (v1.1-v1.x):**
- Real-time collaboration implementation (research only)
- Plugin system implementation (research only)
- Mobile apps (research only)
- Publishing mode (research only)
- Advanced automation beyond current automation engine

---

<details>
<summary>Original Vision (v1.0 - Achieved)</summary>

**One-liner**: Privacy-first, local-only productivity platform replacing fragmented subscription stacks with unified, ethical tooling.

## Problem

Tools that organize our lives have become chaos themselves: scattered across subscriptions, duplicating effort, surrendering data, and chasing features over foundations. Users need a single, trustworthy platform that respects privacy, works offline, and puts the user in control.

## Success Criteria

How we knew it worked:

- [x] Local-first architecture with IndexedDB persistence - Achieved
- [x] Zero telemetry, no server data exfiltration - Achieved
- [x] E2EE backup/export system - Achieved
- [x] Notes, Tasks, Calendar, Time Tracking, Dashboard fully functional - Achieved
- [x] Widget system extensible and performant - Achieved
- [x] Type-safe throughout (TypeScript strict mode) - Achieved
- [x] Bundle size <120KB gzip - Achieved (117KB)
- [x] All P0-P3 features complete - Achieved (100%)

## Constraints

- Privacy-first: Local-first, no telemetry, E2EE backups/exports
- MIT/Apache/BSD licenses only (commercial viability)
- Build features custom, use utility libraries only (80%+ ownership)
- TypeScript strict mode throughout
- Tailwind semantic tokens (no hardcoded colors)
- Performance budgets: <100KB gz initial, <1s load, <100ms interaction

## Out of Scope (v1.0)

- Real-time collaboration
- Plugin ecosystem
- Mobile apps
- Publishing/sharing mode
- AI features beyond terminal integration
- Multi-user/team features
- Cloud sync (local-first only)

</details>

# Roadmap: Neuman Brain Post-P0-P3

## Overview

Transform Neuman Brain from solid foundation (P0-P3 complete) to competitive all-in-one platform through systematic quality refinement, quick-win features, strategic architecture research, and P4 planning. Four-phase approach optimizes for user value delivery (quality + quick wins first) while building knowledge base for future features (research + P4 planning).

## Milestones

- ✅ **v1.0 Foundation** - P0-P3 complete (shipped 2025-11-30)
- 🚧 **v1.1 Quality & Quick Wins** - Phases 1-2 (in progress)
- 📋 **v1.2 Architecture Foundation** - Phases 3-4 (planned)

## Phases

<details>
<summary>✅ v1.0 Foundation (P0-P3) - SHIPPED 2025-11-30</summary>

**Completed Work:**
- P0: Core features (Tasks, Notes, Calendar, Time Tracking, Dashboard)
- P1: Advanced features (Forms, Graph View, Daily Notes, Automation)
- P2: Polish (Gantt export, Recurrence UI, Tag enhancements, Analytics)
- P3: Advanced recurrence patterns

**Outcome:**
- 89,472 lines of TypeScript/React
- 128/128 tests passing
- 117KB gzip bundle size
- Local-first architecture proven
- Zero telemetry, E2EE backups working

</details>

### 🚧 v1.1 Quality & Quick Wins (In Progress)

**Milestone Goal:** Deliver production-ready platform with enhanced UX and high-value quick-win features.

#### Phase 1: Quality & Refinement
**Goal**: Harden platform for production with comprehensive testing, technical debt resolution, and user documentation
**Depends on**: v1.0 foundation complete
**Plans**: 3 plans (E2E tests, technical debt, documentation/UX)

Plans:
- [x] 01-01-PLAN.md: E2E Test Coverage - Playwright tests for all critical user flows (completed 2025-11-30)
- [x] 01-02-PLAN.md: Technical Debt Resolution - IndexedDB optimization, full-text search, bundle analysis (completed 2025-11-30)
- [x] 01-03-PLAN.md: Documentation & UX Polish - User docs, onboarding, UI refinements (completed 2025-12-01)

#### Phase 2: Quick Wins
**Goal**: Ship 6 high-value features that enhance daily usage without architectural complexity
**Depends on**: Phase 1 (quality foundation ensures quick wins don't break)
**Plans**: 9 plans (features split for quality, optimal execution order)

Plans:
- [x] 02-01-PLAN.md: Note Tags - Hashtag syntax, tag filtering, tag management UI (completed 2025-12-01)
- [x] 02-02-PLAN.md: Note Templates Backend - Template CRUD, variable substitution, validation (completed 2025-12-01)
- [x] 02-03-PLAN.md: Note Templates UI - Template library, picker integration, settings (completed 2025-12-01)
- [x] 02-04-PLAN.md: Natural Language Task Dates - Parser & Component (completed 2025-12-01)
- [x] 02-05-PLAN.md: Natural Language Date UI Integration - QuickAddModal, CardDetailPanel, keyboard shortcuts (completed 2025-12-01)
- [x] 02-06-PLAN.md: Markdown Export - Core Utilities (Lexical → MD, Task → MD, ZIP) (completed 2025-12-01)
- [ ] 02-07-PLAN.md: Markdown Export - UI Integration (Notes/Tasks/Settings export UI)
- [ ] 02-08-PLAN.md: Help & Support System - Diagnostic reporting, mailto integration, footer/settings
- [ ] 02-09-PLAN.md: Task Completion Celebrations - Animations, sound effects, achievement tracking

### 📋 v1.2 Architecture Foundation (Planned)

**Milestone Goal:** Establish architectural decisions and research findings to guide P4 feature development.

#### Phase 3: Architecture Research
**Goal**: Systematic evaluation of 4 architectural decisions with evidence, trade-offs, and recommendations
**Depends on**: Phase 2 (quick wins provide real usage data to inform architecture)
**Plans**: 4 plans (one per research domain)

Plans:
- [ ] 03-01-RESEARCH.md → 03-01-FINDINGS.md: Block Editor Evaluation - Notion/Obsidian approaches, library comparison
- [ ] 03-02-RESEARCH.md → 03-02-FINDINGS.md: Graph Database Feasibility - Backlinks/graph view requirements, library options
- [ ] 03-03-RESEARCH.md → 03-03-FINDINGS.md: Local AI Models - On-device processing feasibility, model selection
- [ ] 03-04-RESEARCH.md → 03-04-FINDINGS.md: CRDT Library Selection - Future collaboration requirements, library trade-offs

#### Phase 4: P4 Feature Planning
**Goal**: Detailed planning for 5 long-term features with architecture, milestones, and dependencies documented
**Depends on**: Phase 3 (research findings inform P4 plans)
**Plans**: 5 plans (one per P4 feature)

Plans:
- [ ] 04-01-PLAN.md: Real-time Collaboration Planning - Yjs integration, conflict resolution, sync architecture
- [ ] 04-02-PLAN.md: Plugin System Planning - API design, sandbox security, discovery/distribution
- [ ] 04-03-PLAN.md: Mobile Apps Planning - React Native vs native, sync strategy, offline-first
- [ ] 04-04-PLAN.md: Publishing Mode Planning - Static export, privacy controls, theming
- [ ] 04-05-PLAN.md: Advanced Automation Planning - Service Worker setup, trigger expansion, action library

## Progress

| Phase | Milestone | Plans Complete | Status | Completed |
|-------|-----------|----------------|--------|-----------|
| v1.0 Foundation | v1.0 | P0-P3 | Complete | 2025-11-30 |
| 1. Quality & Refinement | v1.1 | 3/3 | Complete | 2025-12-01 |
| 2. Quick Wins | v1.1 | 6/9 | In progress | - |
| 3. Architecture Research | v1.2 | 0/4 | Not started | - |
| 4. P4 Feature Planning | v1.2 | 0/5 | Not started | - |

## Strategic Notes

**Dependency Rationale:**
- Phase 1 before Phase 2: Quality foundation prevents quick wins from introducing regressions
- Phase 2 before Phase 3: Real usage patterns from quick wins inform architecture research
- Phase 3 before Phase 4: Research findings directly guide P4 feature planning (e.g., CRDT choice affects collaboration plan)

**Value Delivery:**
- v1.1 (Phases 1-2): Immediate user value - production-ready platform + 5 new features
- v1.2 (Phases 3-4): Strategic value - informed decisions for long-term roadmap

**Execution Strategy:**
- Phases 1-2: Execute via `/run-plan` for surgical, focused delivery
- Phase 3: Use RESEARCH.md prompts → FINDINGS.md outputs for systematic evaluation
- Phase 4: Create detailed PLAN.md files for future execution (not immediate implementation)
